candidate_check.description: The Ubuntu operating system must have system commands group-owned by root or a system account.

check_content_excerpt:
Verify the system commands contained in the following directories are group-owned by root or a required system account: 
 
/bin 
/sbin 
/usr/bin 
/usr/sbin 
/usr/local/bin 
/usr/local/sbin 
 
Run the check with the following command: 
 
$ sudo find -L /bin /sbin /usr/bin /usr/sbin /usr/local/bin /usr/local/sbin ! -group root -type f ! -perm /2000 -exec stat -c "%n %G" '{}' \; 
 
If any system commands are returned that are not Set Group ID upon execution (SGID) files and group-owned by a required system account, this is a finding.
fix_text_excerpt:
Configure the system commands to be protected from unauthorized access. Run the following command, replacing "[FILE]" with any system command file not group-owned by "root" or a required system account: 
 
$ sudo chgrp root [FILE]
title: The Ubuntu operating system must have system commands group-owned by root or a system account.
